title: Soulful Japa: Session 54 : Japa is best when uninterrupted
speaker: HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

segments.text: Jayashila Prabhupada, Hare Krishna Nama Prabhuti Jai, welcome all of you for the 44th session
of Soulful Japa. Now, 54th session and yesterday I gave you an introduction to what is tasking,
tasking.
Okay, in our normal language, let me talk. What is this role of counting in Japa? Okay,
let's start with that. I'm just using another word for the whole process, this tasking.
What is a task? Task means, you know, you have a task. From now till you achieve something
you have a task.
Let's say you have to, you have to organize a festival. So there's a task to organize
the festival. That means so many actions are involved all the actions put together connected
together, they become a task. Individual actions become tasks. Just like if you ask a Mason
to build a wall with a brick, brick.
With bricks. As he places brick by brick, each picking each brick and you know placing that in the right place with the mortar is a unit work. It's a unit of work. That is an action.
Action, many many actions put together becomes an activity. So tasking means it's a tasking of an activity with an to achieve something.
Similarly, japa is also not only action of individual chanting come hearing but it is also an activity. That activity is what I am going to define.
That activity is uninterruptedly going on chanting. Uninterruptedly, that is the activity.
Normally, in our mind japa means chanting and hearing, chanting and hearing, chanting and hearing.
This perception we have in our mind.
If I ask you, many of you will say what is japa?
Chanting and hearing, chanting and hearing.
